title: What element of Eva made you decide to check it out
display_title: What element of Eva made you decide to check it out
wikitext:
[]: What element of Eva made you decide to check it out?             Quote Post by shito » Sat Dec 29, 2007 3:37 am Hi all, i'm a new user from Argentina. I read this forum from time to time, same with Eva, I get obsessed from time to time. It's healthier that way :) I guess the poll is clear enough, it is not about your favorite element/aspect of Eva, but the one that made you say "This looks interesting, I'll check it out" in the first place. In my case, it was the mecha/angel designs. The first time I saw something about Evangelion was the opening, as an ad in some Dragon Ball Z video... I thought it was somewhat interesting but the only thing I really remembered from that was Shinji's face at the beginning, and I thought he looked funny (?). Then some weeks/months later I saw another ad in another DBZ video, this time it was the opening music, but with the Sachiel battle scene, and then I thought it was REALLY cool and modern... hehe, back in 1998 Eva looked really modern compared to all the 80s/early 90s anime I was watching. Then I read a small article on a magazine, which basically said it was a post-apocalyptic series with giant robots and unknown enemies called "Angels". And that was it, without knowing much about it I started watching it... thinking it was just a SUPER COOL mecha series with really original/wacky enemy designs. When Lilith appeared in ep 15 and all the stuff going on after that, I knew I wasn't watching just a modern giant robot anime, but something else, and I got totally absorved by it. But truth is, I entered because of the mecha aspect. (I tried to make a poll but it doesn't let me use too much options so it's useless, but these were going to be the options, if you had another one just write it down): - Mecha/Angel designs - Character designs in general - Character designs (girls) - Character designs (boys) - The psychological references - The religious references - I was already a Gainax fan so I had to check it out - Someone introduced it to me - Just the plot ps: I choose mecha/angel designs, because it as the first thing I saw from Eva and decided to look for more, but when I read the small article I became a lot more interested, so the Plot is also another strong reason for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 29, 2007 4:45 am * I was getting back into the habit of watching stuff, grace of DVD drives * I'd exhausted the tolerable looking girls'n'guns titles available at the local Borders * It was becoming available on DVD (this was 4 years ago) * I'd heard of it as having a reputation of being one of the better giant robot shows The rest is history

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Dec 29, 2007 5:34 am I actually first saw Evangelion in manga form. I was in a depressed phase, and just saw a huge amount of parallels between myself and Shinji. I then properly started watching the anime when I became interested in psychology anfd philosophy - or rather, I could say that Evangelion got me interested in psychology.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Dec 29, 2007 6:26 am Hmmm, interesting.... I was reading a few mangas that had some Eva things on it as a homage, and I decided that I might as well as get the whole thing, and after watching it, I became a fervent fan. Also, some other Wagnerites persuaded me to watch it, them knowing I like anime and mangas in general.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Dec 29, 2007 9:28 am It was the fifth anime I saw after Cowboy Bebop, Dragonball Z, Outlaw Star, and Noir. I got one of those Amazon.com email recommendations, saying that Evangelion was one of the best anime ever. So, I downloaded it and, since I had no expectations or no idea what it was even about, I ended up loving it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sat Dec 29, 2007 10:30 am Went and saw The End of Evangelion at the movies. Laughed but got intrigued.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Dec 29, 2007 3:36 pm none I downloaded eva after a random search for the word " anime " on kazaa and got hooked. How that didn't lead me to more porn I will never know

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 29, 2007 4:01 pm Sibs told me to watch it.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Sat Dec 29, 2007 4:53 pm DatDude wrote: none I downloaded eva after a random search for the word " anime " on kazaa and got hooked. How that didn't lead me to more porn I will never know

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Sat Dec 29, 2007 5:49 pm psycho-logi-cal Not in having to do with any *specific* psychological theory, but in just hearing that the series had a lot of powerful characterization going on. Often to do with angst and trauma, but that's always the way. I think it was an article in Anime Insider that led me to check it out for these reasons, though at the time I was at least enough into anime to have seen *images* from the series without any clue as to what it was about.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Dec 29, 2007 5:55 pm I had often heard of it being considered the best anime series, and the only two GAINAX series I had seen before (FLCL and Mahoromatic ) had affected me more than any other anime, so it was an obvious decision to watch it when it started on Adult Swim. After Magma Diver , though, I got fed up with waiting a week between episodes and got Netflix. I watched the rest of the series within a few days with the plan that lets you have three DVDs out at a time.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Dec 29, 2007 5:58 pm A pretty box and me watching Nadia before hand. I liked Nadia a lot so I though Ganiax was a pretty good company. I saw Eva was made by them too and it even had #1 anime of all time on the box so I got it.

[]:             Quote Post by darkness713 » Sat Dec 29, 2007 6:54 pm Pathetic as it may sound, the psycological points of view and such in the anime helped get me out of my depression. I didn't quite understand it the first time through, but after studying it, it began to make sense :P . I also love the mech designs, the plot, and the character designs (particularly Rei :P).

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Dec 29, 2007 7:45 pm What element of Eva made you decide to check it out?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Dec 29, 2007 8:00 pm Rami-san's Inner Torus Reactor! Nuclear Powered Angel Death Rays are WIN!!11!!!1 Seriously, it was purely by accident I caught onto an Eva fansite, and found it all very interesting. After I got sick of Halo, this was the place to be!

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sat Dec 29, 2007 8:12 pm A friend showed me End of Evangelion back when it was released. I had no idea what was going on, but I thought it was pretty cool, so I eventually checked out the rest of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Sat Dec 29, 2007 8:42 pm I knew what the show was about and it intrigued me from a distance. I finally decided to check it out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 29, 2007 8:52 pm BobBQ wrote: The Eva-sized Desert Eagle from E16.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Dec 30, 2007 11:14 am @ Reichu He could have said misatos bouncing boobies. Would that have been better :)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Dec 30, 2007 11:31 am Actually, I'm surprised BobBQ went for the Desert Eagle lookalike. I'd have taken the Mechnem NTW-20 lookalike sniper that Rei uses. Reichu wrote: :facepalms, deeply:

It's healthier that way :) I guess the poll is clear enough, it is not about your favorite element/aspect of Eva, but the one that made you say "This looks interesting, I'll check it
